segments.text: Today we're going to look at an important area of science, namely nanotechnology.
segments.translation: 今天我们来看一门重要的科学，也就是所谓的纳米技术。

segments.text: So what is it?
segments.translation: 所以它是什么呢？

segments.text: Nano means tiny, so it's science and engineering on the scale of atoms and molecules.
segments.translation: 纳米的意思是微小，所以是原子级和分子级的科学和工程。

segments.text: The idea is that by controlling and rearranging atoms, you can literally create anything.
segments.translation: 在于通过控制和重新排列原子，你真的可以创造任何东西。

segments.text: However, as we'll see, the science of the small has some big implications affecting us in many ways.
segments.translation: 但是，正如我们所见，关于微小物质的科学却给我们从很多方面带来大大的影响。

segments.text: There's no doubt that nanotechnology promises so much for civilisation.
segments.translation: 无疑纳米技术给文明带来太多希望。

segments.text: However, all new technologies have their teething problems.
segments.translation: 但是，所有新的技术都有它们萌芽阶段的问题。

segments.text: And with nanotechnology, society often gets the wrong idea about its capabilities.
segments.translation: 对于纳米技术，社会通常对它的能力有着错误的想法。

segments.text: Numerous science-fiction books and movies have raised people's fears about nanotechnology - with scenarios such as inserting little nano-robots into your body that monitor everything you do without you realising it, or self-replicating nano-robots that eventually take over the world.
segments.translation: 无数的科幻书籍和电影引发了人们对于纳米技术的恐惧。比如在你不知情的情况下，在你的身体里植入纳米机器人来监视你做的一切，或者自我复制的纳米机器人最终统治了世界。

segments.text: So how do we safeguard such a potentially powerful technology?
segments.translation: 所以我们怎样能保护一项可能如此强大的技术不被滥用？

segments.text: Some scientists recommend that nano-particles be treated as new chemicals with separate safety tests and clear labelling.
segments.translation: 有些科学家建议纳米颗粒应该被当做化学品，需要单独的安全测试和清晰的标注。

segments.text: They believe that greater care should also be taken with nano-particles in laboratories and factories.
segments.translation: 他们相信在实验室和工厂里，应该给予纳米颗粒更多的关注。

segments.text: Others have called for a withdrawal of new nano products such as cosmetics and a temporary halt to many kinds of nanotech research.
segments.translation: 其他人则呼吁收回新的纳米产品，比如化妆品还要临时终止各种纳米技术的研究。

segments.text: But as far as I'm concerned there's a need to plough ahead with the discoveries and applications of nanotechnology.
segments.translation: 但是，在我看来，我们需要继续发掘纳米技术的发现和应用。

segments.text: I really believe that most scientists would welcome a way to guard against unethical uses of such technology.
segments.translation: 我确信大部分科学家会欢迎这类技术的保护措施，防止滥用。

segments.text: We can't go around thinking that all innovation is bad, all advancement is bad.
segments.translation: 我们不能总认为所有的创新都是不好的，所有的发展都是不好的。

segments.text: As with the debate about any new technology, it is how you use it that's important.
segments.translation: 就像所有有关新技术的辩论，怎样使用它才是最重要的。

segments.text: So let's look at some of its possible uses.
segments.translation: 所以让我们来看看可能的利用方式。

segments.text: Thanks to nanotechnology, there could be a major breakthrough in the field of transportation with the production of more durable metals.
segments.translation: 多亏有了纳米技术，更耐用的金属出现，实现了交通领域突破，。

segments.text: These could be virtually unbreakable, lighter and much more pliable leading to planes that are 50 times lighter than at present.
segments.translation: 事实上它们可以不易破损，重量更轻，而且更柔韧，所以这种材质让飞机可以比现在轻50倍。

segments.text: Those same improved capabilities will dramatically reduce the cost of travelling into space making it more accessible to ordinary people and opening up a totally new holiday destination.
segments.translation: 与此同时，提升了的性能使空间旅行的成本会大大减少，普通人也更容易接触到它。这开发了一个全新的度假目的地。

segments.text: In terms of technology, the computer industry will be able to shrink computer parts down to minute sizes.
segments.translation: 从技术角度而言，电脑行业可以把电脑零件缩小的极小的尺寸。

segments.text: We need nanotechnology in order to create a new generation of computers that will work even faster and will have a million times more memory but will be about the size of a sugar cube.
segments.translation: 我们需要利用纳米科技来创造新一代的电脑，运转速度更快，储存空间扩大几百万倍，但电脑的大小却只有一颗方糖那么大。

segments.text: Nanotechnology could also revolutionise the way that we generate power.
segments.translation: 纳米技术还促使对发电的方式进行革命性改变。

segments.text: The cost of solar cells will be drastically reduced so harnessing this energy will be far more economical than at present.
segments.translation: 太阳能电池的成本会大幅下降，所以利用这项能源会比现在节约更多成本。

segments.text: But nanotechnology has much wider applications than this and could have an enormous impact on our environment.
segments.translation: 但是纳米技术还有更多的应用，可以对我们的环境产生巨大的影响。

segments.text: For instance, tiny airborne nano-robots could be programmed to actually rebuild the ozone layer, which could lessen the impact of global warming on our planet.
segments.translation: 比如，微小的空中纳米机器人可以设计为去修复臭氧层，这样可以减少我们的星球上全球变暖的影响。

segments.text: That's a pretty amazing thought, isn't it?
segments.translation: 这是不是很神奇？

segments.text: On a more local scale, this new technology could help with the clean-up of environmental disasters as nanotechnology will allow us to remove oil and other contaminants from the water far more effectively.
segments.translation: 从更小的范围去看，这项新的技术可以帮助清理环境灾难，因为纳米技术可以让我们从水中更高效的去除油污和其它污染物。

segments.text: And, if nanotechnology progresses as expected - as a sort of building block set of about 90 atoms - then you could build anything you wanted from the bottom up.
segments.translation: 而且，如果纳米技术如期发展，有了由90个原子的一块的标准部件，你可以自下而上地搭建任何东西。

segments.text: In terms of production, this means that you only use what you need and so there wouldn't be any waste.
segments.translation: 从生产方面来说，这就意味着你只使用你需要的原料，而不会产生任何废物。

segments.text: The notion that you could create anything at all has major implications for our health.
segments.translation: 你可以创造任何东西的，这对于我们的健康也有着影响。

segments.text: It means that we'll eventually be able to replicate anything.
segments.translation: 这就意味着我们最终可以复制任何东西。

segments.text: This would have a phenomenal effect on our society.
segments.translation: 这会让我们的社会产生巨大的变化。

segments.text: In time it could even lead to the eradication of famine through the introduction of machines that produce food to feed the hungry.
segments.translation: 届时它甚至可以借助引入生产食物的机器来喂养饥饿的人，以此消除饥荒。

segments.text: But it's in the area of medicine that nanotechnology may have its biggest impact.
segments.translation: 但是纳米技术应该在制药业有着它最大的影响。

segments.text: How we detect disease will change as tiny biosensors are developed to analyse tests in minutes rather than days.
segments.translation: 随着微小的生物探测器的开发，疾病的检测方式也会发生改变。不需要几天的等待，几分钟就分析出测试结果。

segments.text: There's even speculation nano-robots could be used to slow the ageing process, lengthening life expectancy.
segments.translation: 甚至还可以利用具有推断能力的纳米机器人减缓衰老，延长我们的预期寿命。

segments.text: As you can see, I'm very excited by the implications that could be available to us in the next few decades.
segments.translation: 正如你们所见，我对于在未来几十年纳米带给我们的影响十分兴奋。

segments.text: Just how long it'll take, I honestly don't know.
segments.translation: 只是我真的不知道这到底要花多久。
